title: judgment record — records/judgments/JDG-NNNN.json
description: One judgment, one owned file (C17) — the unified ledger that replaces the shared signoff.json. Kinds: 'sign-off' (a dated human judgment satisfying a manual rule), 'deviation' (an accepted rule violation), 'risk-acceptance' (an accepted risk with expiry), 'break-glass' (an expiring override of a fail-closed admit/reconcile gate; FS5: it must land on main via its own prior PR — enforcement is M6's job, expressibility is this schema's). Risk acceptance is dated human judgment (C08); expected_state snapshots the world the judgment assumed, tripwire is the machine-evaluable condition that voids it, review_by expires it. The evaluator ships in M4b (`baseline jdg check`); the reconcile sweep that fires tripwires on cron is M6.

id.description: The record's identity — must match the filename.

kind.description: What species of judgment this is. A break-glass additionally carries 'gate'.

date.description: When the judgment was made. A judgment is dated or it is not a judgment.

by.description: Who judged — a person, never an agent rubber-stamp.

subject.description: What is judged: a rule id (TEST-03), a file (baseline.repo.json — the DESC-03 shape), or a scope string.

reason.description: The rationale — why this deviation/risk/override is acceptable. Exists in no diff.

review_by.description: Expiry: the date by which this judgment must be re-made or it lapses. Every judgment expires — that is what keeps the ledger from fossilizing.

expected_state.description: Snapshot of the world the judgment assumed: a map of dotted fact path -> expected value (e.g. {"descriptor.maturity": "prototype"}). Machine-comparable by `baseline jdg check` (M4b).

tripwire.description: The condition that VOIDS this judgment when it becomes true — how the engine detects the accepted world changed (C08). Evaluated by M4b's `baseline jdg check`; swept on cron by M6's reconcile.

fact.description: Dotted derived-fact path (same namespace expected_state uses).

value.description: Comparand for eq/ne/gt/lt — any JSON value.

gate.description: break-glass only: which fail-closed gate this record relieves (FS5). Ignored for other kinds until M4b's per-kind laws tighten it.
